name: Remote: Remote Desktop Access from the Internet
notes: This rule triggers when the Microsoft Remote Desktop Protocol is detected from the Internet to a local host.  Most companies consider this a violation of corporate policy.  If this is normal activity on your network, you should disable this rule.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Remote Desktop Access from a Remote Host
conditions_text: when a flow matches all of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Remote Desktop Access from a Remote Host, BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication
TD_list: APPLY Remote: Remote Desktop Access from the Internet on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when a flow matches all of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Remote Desktop Access from a Remote Host, BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication
RED_list: Event name: Remote Desktop Access from the Internet

Event description: Detects the Microsoft Remote Desktop Protocol from the Internet to a local host.  Most companies consider this a violation of corporate policy.  If this is normal activity on your network, you should disable this rule.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
